researched_category.notes: Front is a customer operations platform with a shared inbox, omnichannel communication (email, SMS, chat, social), workflow automation, and AI-powered features.

one_liner.value: Customer operations platform that provides a shared inbox for teams to manage, triage, and respond to customer communications across email, chat, SMS, and social channels.

methods.notes: Front supports OAuth2.0 (authorization code grant) and API tokens (Bearer tokens). OAuth is required for public integrations; API tokens are for testing/internal use. The MCP server uses OAuth 2.1 + PKCE.

access.notes: A free developer account can be created at dev.frontapp.com with no payment required. For API token creation in a production Front instance, you must be a company admin. For the MCP server, you need to create an OAuth app (requires admin role in Front). The developer account is free and self-serve; production plans start at $25/seat/mo but API access itself does not require a paid plan for development.

summary.value: Front's Core API is a comprehensive REST API covering conversations, messages, contacts, accounts, channels, inboxes, teammates, comments, drafts, tags, rules, time off, analytics exports, and more. Additional APIs exist for Channels (custom messaging), Plugin SDK (UI extensions), Connectors (no-code external API calls), Links, Chat Widget, and Autopilot Resolve SDK. Webhooks are supported for real-time events. The API has granular rate limits (50-200 rpm depending on plan) and supports cursor-based pagination.

agent_interface.notes: Front has an official MCP server (open beta) at mcp.frontapp.com/mcp, documented at dev.frontapp.com/docs/mcp-server. It uses OAuth 2.1 + PKCE, supports Streamable HTTP transport, and provides per-user-scoped access to Front conversation data. The MCP server is in open beta and listed on the Front developer portal as a core product offering.

rationale.value: Front has a well-documented public REST API (Core API), free self-serve developer accounts, multiple authentication options (OAuth2 recommended for agent toolkit, API tokens for testing), and an official MCP server in open beta. The API is broad with comprehensive coverage of Front's data model. The main consideration is that API tokens require admin privileges to create in a Front instance, and for agent toolkits targeting multi-tenant use, OAuth is the recommended path (required for public integrations). The MCP server is beta but functional.

s1.quote: Front is the customer operations platform built for B2B complexity, keeping every team, tool, and customer conversation in sync so companies can scale without losing connection.

s2.quote: All-in-one support platform. Essential capabilities to get started with single-channel support.

s3.quote: Front supports OAuth2.0 as well as API Tokens. To learn about authenticating with Front, see our guide on authentication.

s4.quote: There are two ways to get a Bearer token — through API tokens in your settings or through an OAuth implementation. In general, Front recommends implementing OAuth when feasible.

s5.quote: You must be an admin to create or manage API tokens.

s6.quote: You can create a developer account by signing up here. You’ll get access to a free developer environment that will provide everything you need to begin building with our APIs. Developer accounts are limited to 3 teammates.

s7.quote: Follow the instructions on the Developer Portal to create a free developer account to begin building integrations on the Front Platform.

s8.quote: If you don't already have an account, you'll want to sign up for a free development account.

s9.quote: By default, Front's API rate limit starts at 50 requests per minute and varies depending on your plan. Starter: 50 rpm, Professional: 100 rpm, Enterprise: 200 rpm.

s10.quote: Core API: Sync data between Front and external systems and perform backend operations that retrieve, create, modify, or delete resources in Front.

s11.quote: Front's MCP server (mcp.frontapp.com/mcp) lets AI agents act on Front conversation data using a single OAuth-authenticated endpoint.

s12.quote: We're excited to announce the beta release of the Front MCP server — and we'd love for you to try it.

s13.quote: Front's MCP server (mcp.frontapp.com/mcp) lets AI agents act on Front conversation data using a single OAuth-authenticated endpoint. Endpoint: https://mcp.frontapp.com/mcp. Transport: Streamable HTTP. MCP spec version: 2025-11-25. Auth: OAuth 2.1 + PKCE.
